description: Case handles used by the adversarial coverage matrix. These are task anchors, not a generated manifest.

cases.claim: Python multi-clause comprehension, nested append loop, and JS flatMap/map compute the same flattened list.
cases.evidence: Focused verify corpus after PR #66: no false merges and complete behavior-equal convergence.

cases.claim: A flattened list is not a nested list of lists.
cases.mutation: FlatMap[A, lambda a. Map[B, lambda b. e]] -> Map[A, lambda a. Map[B, lambda b. e]]
cases.evidence: The #57 equivalence test asserts flat-map and nested-list fingerprints differ.

cases.claim: Mapping to a list without flattening is not one-level flat-map.
cases.mutation: flatMap callback result is kept nested
cases.evidence: Required sibling for any future FlatMap idiom extension.

cases.claim: filter_map-style option-producing callbacks can converge with explicit filter+map where None drops elements.
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test and hof_filter_map smoke converge Python filtered comprehension, JS filter().map(), Rust filter_map Some/None, and Rust guarded Vec::new()/push builder.

cases.claim: Dropping None differs from mapping None as a value.
cases.mutation: filter_map callback returns None where map keeps a sentinel value
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test rejects a Rust map that emits Option payloads instead of dropping None.

cases.claim: Changing the mapped Some value changes behavior even when the predicate stays the same.
cases.mutation: Some(x) -> Some(x + 1)
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test rejects Some(x * 3) against the covered Some(x * 2) value coordinate.

cases.claim: filter_map keeps falsey Some-like values; only Null/None absence drops an item.
cases.mutation: coerce the callback result through truthiness before deciding whether to emit it
cases.evidence: Interpreter FilterMap tests keep Int(0), and focused equivalence test converges Some(0) with a filtered zero map while rejecting a later truthy zero filter.

cases.claim: Richer option-producing match/and_then forms may converge with explicit filter+map when they preserve the same absence and emitted-value coordinates.
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test and hof_filter_map smoke converge Python/TypeScript filter+map with Rust match-guard and pure Option::and_then filter_map callbacks.

cases.claim: A wrapped None payload is an emitted value in richer option domains, not the current Null absence sentinel.
cases.mutation: Some(None)-style payload vs None absence
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test converges Rust Some(None) payloads with Python filtered None values and rejects them against mapped x*2 values.

cases.claim: A sum over a flat-map stream equals the corresponding nested reduction loop.
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test converges Python sum over a flat-map generator, Python nested reduction loop, and JS flatMap().reduce().

cases.claim: A max selection over a flat-map stream equals the corresponding nested selection loop.
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test converges max(x + y for x in xs for y in ys) with the nested best-update loop.

cases.claim: An any/existence reduction over a flat-map stream equals the corresponding nested early-return loop.
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test converges any(x + y > 0 for x in xs for y in ys) with a nested early-return existence loop.

cases.claim: Changing the reduction seed changes aggregate behavior.
cases.mutation: sum seed 0 -> 1
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test keeps a nested sum loop seeded with 1 distinct from the flat-map sum seeded with 0.

cases.claim: Aggregating a nested list shape differs from aggregating a flattened stream unless a separate flatten is proven.
cases.mutation: flat stream -> nested list
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test keeps sum over nested list rows distinct from sum over the flattened stream.

cases.claim: Changing the flattened any predicate changes behavior.
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test keeps the inverted predicate distinct from the covered > predicate.

cases.claim: A flat-map aggregate whose emitted value ignores the outer element still depends on the outer collection cardinality.
cases.mutation: sum(y for x in xs for y in ys) -> sum(y for y in ys)
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test keeps the outer-ignored flat-map aggregate and nested loop distinct from the direct inner sum.

cases.claim: A filtered flat-map aggregate may converge with an equivalent nested guarded reduction when outer and inner predicates are preserved.
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test converges Python filtered flat-map sum, Python nested guarded sum loop, JS filtered flatMap().reduce(), and JS method-terminal .some()/.every() with the equivalent Python filtered any/all generators.

cases.claim: Changing an outer or inner FlatMap aggregate predicate changes behavior.
cases.mutation: outer/inner/terminal predicate changed to a static false predicate while contribution stays the same
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test keeps static-false outer predicates, inner predicates, and method-terminal predicates distinct from the covered filtered FlatMap aggregate.

cases.claim: A filtered FlatMap any/all existence or universal aggregate may converge with the equivalent nested guarded early-return loop.
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence test converges Python filtered any/all generators, JS filtered flatMap().some()/every(), and equivalent Python nested guarded early-return loops.

cases.claim: A pure Java Stream.flatMap chain can express the same one-level flat-map as Python multi-clause comprehension.
cases.evidence: Focused Java stream corpus verifies with 4/4 interpretable units, zero false merges, and complete behavior-equal convergence; the source-level equivalence test asserts Java Stream.flatMap/map matches Python multi-clause comprehension.

cases.claim: Java Stream.map returning streams is not Stream.flatMap.
cases.mutation: flatMap -> map
cases.evidence: The focused corpus includes nestedMapJava and nested_map_py as the nested-shape sibling, and the source-level equivalence test asserts nested-map fingerprints differ from flat-map fingerprints.

cases.claim: Effectful stream callbacks cannot be treated as pure HoF transformations.
cases.mutation: lambda mutates external state or performs an effect
cases.evidence: The Java stream oracle tests execute a FlatMap callback containing a print effect and assert the effect trace is preserved, so callback effects remain observable and outside the pure idiom rule.

cases.claim: Clamp-shaped min/max compositions converge when numeric lo<=hi is proven.
cases.evidence: Proof-backed integer min/max compositions now converge through Clamp when an exiting inverse guard or ordered literal bounds prove lo<=hi. Focused smoke: numeric_clamp positive recall 1/1.

cases.claim: Clamp composition is not sound when lo<=hi is not proven.
cases.mutation: remove or invert the bound-order proof
cases.evidence: Focused tests reject missing and non-exiting guards, swapped bounds, wrong nesting, unproven ternary clamps, and unproven Rust .clamp(lo, hi); numeric_clamp smoke reports 0/4 hard-negative false merges and the bridge corpus verifies with 0 false merges.

cases.claim: Floating-point NaN/min/max behavior is not covered by integer clamp facts.
cases.mutation: numeric domain becomes floatish
cases.evidence: Float-annotated clamp min/max and ternary forms remain outside the integer Clamp canon; numeric_clamp smoke includes a float boundary hard negative and the bridge corpus verifies with 0 false merges.

cases.claim: A two-comparison clamp can converge with proof-backed min/max clamp composition under the same integer lo<=hi proof.
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence tests and the bridge corpus show guarded lower-first and upper-first Python ternary clamps converging with proof-backed min/max Clamp while unproven, swapped-bound, and float ternaries stay split.

cases.claim: Language library clamp forms can converge with proof-backed min/max clamp composition when the same integer bound proof is available.
cases.evidence: Rust numeric .clamp now converges with literal ordered min/max clamps and with guarded Rust x.max(lo).min(hi) under the same lo<=hi proof. The bridge corpus verifies with 0 false merges.

cases.claim: A method named clamp is not a numeric library clamp without a proven numeric receiver and bound-order proof.
cases.mutation: custom receiver method or parameter bounds without lo<=hi proof
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence tests keep unproven Rust x.clamp(lo, hi) and custom Wrap::clamp forms split from literal/proof-backed Clamp values; the bridge corpus verifies with 0 false merges.

cases.claim: Imported immutable literal map lookup with default can converge with local literal/default forms when provenance is proven.
cases.evidence: Semantic scan groups local.py and imported.py after Python sibling import provenance resolves the unmutated literal provider.

cases.claim: Receiver mutation between binding/import and lookup changes behavior.
cases.mutation: insert write to receiver before lookup
cases.evidence: Provider and importer mutation fixtures stay out of the imported/local semantic family across Python and JS imports.

cases.claim: Looking up the same key/default on a different map is behaviorally different.
cases.mutation: receiver map coordinate changes
cases.evidence: Existing map-default tests plus cross-file Python, JS, Java, and Rust wrong-map fixtures keep changed present-key values split.

cases.claim: Imported JavaScript/TypeScript literal map exports can converge with local map-default forms when module/export provenance is proven.
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence tests and the map_default_non_python_import corpus prove JS/TS named imports from unmutated sibling map exports converge with local literal map-default lookup.

cases.claim: Imported Java static literal map bindings can converge with local map-default forms when static import provenance is proven.
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence tests and the map_default_non_python_import corpus prove Java static imports from unmutated class Map.of fields converge with local literal map-default lookup.

cases.claim: Imported Rust const map-entry arrays can converge with local map-default forms when module/export provenance is proven.
cases.evidence: Focused equivalence tests and the map_default_non_python_import corpus prove Rust use-imported const entry arrays consumed by HashMap::from converge with local literal map-default lookup.

cases.claim: HoF oracle behavior must preserve lambda error propagation and empty-collection behavior.
cases.evidence: Interpreter tests cover Map/Filter/FlatMap/FilterMap lambda errors, empty collections, and FilterMap capture/null behavior; the focused HoF oracle corpus verifies with zero false merges.

cases.claim: FlatMap callback results must be lists; non-list callback results are Err in the oracle.
cases.mutation: callback returns scalar
cases.evidence: Interpreter tests assert scalar FlatMap lambda results are Err; the focused corpus carries a scalar flatMap fixture.

cases.claim: Effectful callbacks cannot be folded into pure value-graph HoF facts without effect modeling.
cases.mutation: callback emits or mutates
cases.evidence: Interpreter tests assert FilterMap callback print effects remain observable and ordered; the hof_filter_map corpus carries an effectful Rust filter_map callback that the value graph must leave opaque.

cases.claim: Deep HoF chains should retain compact value fingerprints.
cases.evidence: Perf smoke candidate for future broad iterator fusion.

cases.claim: Wide generated HoF expressions should not explode value-node count or scan runtime.
cases.evidence: Perf hard-negative for representation growth.
